title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. primigenius
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. primigenius, commonly referred to as wild daffodil or Lent lily, is a subspecies of the narcissus plant native to the United Kingdom. This perennial plant has medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of wild daffodil include its potential to reduce inflammation, boost immune function, and exhibit antiviral properties, making it a valuable herb for traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for the treatment of various health conditions, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
